name: Global Business Travel Group, Inc.
business: Global Business Travel Group, Inc. (Amex GBT) is a technology and services company for travel, expense, and meetings & events, offering a marketplace that connects business travelers with travel suppliers. The company derives revenue from transaction fees, supplier incentives, and product and professional services, with no single client exceeding 2% of revenue. As of December 31, 2025, it had over 27,000 employees and operations in 49 countries.
sellsTo: business clients of all sizes (SMEs and large enterprises)
travel suppliers (airlines, hotels, car rental, rail, etc.)

exposures.sentence: American Express Global Business Travel, operated by Global Business Travel Group, Inc., a Delaware corporation, ("Amex GBT") is a leading technology and services company for travel, expense, and meetings & events.

Our comprehensive and competitive marketplace, industry-leading software, AI (as defined herein)-powered efficiencies and 24/7 global support team offer solutions, savings, and flexibility for companies of every size.

Additionally, our clients benefit from heightened BHC level compliance standards, proactive risk management across all risk categories and robust governance and control frameworks encompassing cybersecurity, data protection and third-party oversight programs.

catalysts.sentence: On September 2, 2025, we completed the acquisition of CWT in accordance with terms of agreement.
On December 29, 2025, we gained control over Uvet Global Business Travel S.p.A. ("Uvet GBT"), by obtaining majority representation on its board of directors.

catalysts.event: Strategic alliance with SAP Concur to co-develop Complete platform
catalysts.sentence: In October 2025, Amex GBT announced a strategic alliance with SAP to co-develop a new integrated AI-powered platform called Complete, which will combine booking, servicing, payments and expense integration into one experience.

catalysts.event: Next-gen launch of Egencia integrating Concur Expense
catalysts.sentence: We are also integrating Concur Expense with Amex GBT Egencia as part of a next-gen launch in 2026 with new AI features and user experience to capture the large and profitable SME opportunity.

catalysts.event: Amended credit agreement to reduce interest rate margin
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, we amended the A&R Credit Agreement to reduce the interest rate margin on term loans from 3.00% per annum to 2.50% per annum.

catalysts.event: Further amendment to credit agreement with additional borrowing
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we entered into a further amendment to the A&R Credit Agreement, whereby we reduced the margin on the Term B-1 Loans by 50 basis points and borrowed an additional principal amount of term loans of $100 million (see note 25 - Subsequent Events to our consolidated financial statement included elsewhere in this Annual Report).

catalysts.event: Share repurchase program increased to $600 million
catalysts.sentence: On February 17, 2026, we announced that our Board of Directors authorized an increase of the amount available for the share repurchase program from $300 million to $600 million.

tamClaims.claim: Global business travel was estimated to be a $1.57 trillion industry in 2025.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the GBTA, global business travel was estimated to be a $1.57 trillion industry in 2025 and global business travel spend is projected to surpass $2 trillion by 2029 ("2025 Business Travel Index Outlook," July 2025).

tamClaims.claim: Global business travel spend is projected to surpass $2 trillion by 2029.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the GBTA, global business travel was estimated to be a $1.57 trillion industry in 2025 and global business travel spend is projected to surpass $2 trillion by 2029 ("2025 Business Travel Index Outlook," July 2025).

tamClaims.claim: Top 10 TMCs accounted for approximately $99 billion in business travel TTV in 2024, less than 6% of total spend.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that the top 10 TMCs in aggregate accounted for approximately $99 billion in business travel TTV in 2024, or less than 6% of total business travel spend worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: Travel and expense management software was a $2.9 billion industry in 2023 by revenue.
tamClaims.sentence: According to International Data Corporation, travel and expense management software was estimated as a $2.9 billion industry in 2023 by revenue.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 30-40% of business travel spend in the United States and Europe was managed in 2024.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that approximately 30-40% of business travel spend in the United States and Europe was managed in 2024.
